title: Operator brief structure and presentation
summary: New operator-facing narrative artifacts MUST use the shared brief data contract and render to self-contained semantic HTML; legacy Markdown and worker-control records remain valid exceptions.
instructions: New operator-facing narrative artifacts MUST be authored as schema-valid brief data and rendered to HTML with the Pancreator brief system. Existing Markdown artifacts do not require migration.
A brief MUST begin with an executive-summary section that states the bottom line first: what changed or matters, why it matters, and outcomes or required actions when applicable.
Brief data MUST describe content and semantic intent; layout, typography, color, spacing, and responsive behavior MUST remain in the shared or project design system.
Section headings MUST use concise titles and a registered semantic key. Renderers MUST resolve the heading emoji from that registry rather than accepting ad hoc emoji choices.
Within one repository, each registered section emoji MUST retain one meaning. Project definitions MUST extend rather than override Pancreator-owned semantic keys.

Cards SHOULD separate metadata from substantive detail through field placement, SHOULD expose clear actions when action is possible, and MUST avoid unnecessary nesting, decorative clutter, and raw-log substitution.

Canonical invocation and delegation cards, source documentation, machine-readable workflow records, and legacy artifacts MAY remain Markdown or JSON when those formats are part of their execution contract.
